_comment: Copy this file to config.json and set PIXAI_API_KEY. config.json is git-ignored and will never be committed.
_comment_api_key: THE ONLY VALUE YOU NEED. Your official API key from platform.pixai.art (lifetime up to ~2 years). Sent as the Bearer credential for ALL calls. Your USER_ID is auto-resolved from it, and the persisted-query hashes below ship with working defaults -- so with just this key, everything works. Nothing else here is required unless a default stops working.
_comment_optional: EVERYTHING BELOW IS OPTIONAL. Leave as-is. The hashes are public identifiers of PixAI's own frontend GraphQL ops (not secrets, not per-user); they ship baked-in and only need a value here if PixAI overhauls their frontend and a built-in default goes stale (you'll get a clear 'recapture' error if so).
_comment_user_id: Optional override. Auto-resolved from the API key via the `me` query; only set this to pin a specific id.
_comment_hashes: Optional overrides for the baked-in persisted-query hashes (feed / task-detail / delete). Capture from DevTools -> Network -> graphql -> the matching request -> Payload -> extensions.persistedQuery.sha256Hash, only if a default rotates.
_comment_read_only: Set true to refuse every credit-spending or account-deleting call outright -- CLI or web, and even with --confirm/--apply/--yes passed. A safety net for a first run, testing, or handing the tool to someone else. See the Trust & Safety wiki page.
_comment_legacy: DEPRECATED legacy fallback for users WITHOUT an API key. U3T is the 'u3t' field from a graphql request Payload; the short-lived Bearer token then goes in token.txt / PIXAI_TOKEN / --token and must be re-captured every few hours. Leave blank when using PIXAI_API_KEY (recommended).
_comment_auth: Session-based login for the web gallery. Routes split into three tiers, NOT a blanket 'login gates every non-localhost request': a handful are PUBLIC with no session at all (the login page, logout, /branding, the PWA manifest); most need any LOGIN session (local or LAN); a few destructive/config-writing routes are LOCALHOST-only even for a logged-in LAN session. See tests/test_route_tiers.py and docs/architecture.md. Neither field below is something you type in by hand.
_comment_auth_secret: AUTH_SECRET_KEY signs the Flask session cookie. Auto-generated (secrets.token_hex(32)) and written here the first time the gallery server starts if this is blank/absent -- never set it yourself.
_comment_auth_users: AUTH_USERS is a list of {"username": ..., "password_hash": ...} accounts for logging into the gallery from another device on your LAN. Managed via the CLI (python pixai_gallery_backup.py --add-web-user (interactive, hashes with werkzeug) / --remove-web-user <username> / --list-web-users) -- but NOT only the CLI: the first-run web bootstrap creates an account when AUTH_USERS is empty, and once logged in, Control Panel -> Users can add/remove accounts too (removing an account that isn't your own needs the server's own machine). Never edit this by hand and never paste a plaintext password here. Empty (the default) means LAN login is impossible until an account exists.
_comment_auth_epoch: AUTH_EPOCH_SEQ is an install-wide monotonic counter the app writes itself (every /logout bumps it) to support session revocation -- never set it yourself. A legacy config with no existing value gets a 1,000,000 margin on first mint so cookies issued before this existed can't be walked back through. See docs/STATE.md's Web auth section.
